content:
{
  "title": "Function-Aware Prompting: Structuring Code Prompts with Contextual API Signatures",
  "problem": "Current prompting methods for code generation treat prompts as linear text, often failing to surface or emphasize the structure and constraints of the underlying APIs or functions. This can lead to syntactically valid but semantically incorrect or inefficient code completions.",
  "motivation": "As LLMs become increasingly deployed in code generation tasks, precision and reliability are paramount, especially for professional software engineering workflows. Improving prompt design to better guide models toward correct, context-aware code can reduce post-editing and increase trust in LLM-assisted coding. Recent advances in model capacity make it timely to experiment with richer prompt structures.",
  "method": "We propose Function-Aware Prompting, which prepends the prompt with formal API or function signatures—including argument types, docstrings, and type constraints—encoded in a standardized, token-efficient format. The prompt is dynamically constructed so that every request to generate code for a function includes an explicit, machine-readable schema of the function's interface and constraints. This method leverages LLMs’ ability to reason over structured context, providing more direct guidance and disambiguation than natural language-only prompts. We will explore variants that interleave signature tokens with natural language intent descriptions and compare against standard code prompting.",
  "experiment_plan": "We will evaluate on HumanEval, MBPP, and APPS datasets. Baselines will include vanilla natural language prompts and chain-of-thought prompting. Metrics will include pass@k, syntactic correctness, and semantic alignment with function intent. Ablation studies will test the impact of schema detail and interleaved prompt structure."
}
